question: Who is the author of You Can Combat Computer Stress!?  the author refers to au_lname; You Can Combat Computer Stress! refers to title = 'You Can Combat Computer Stress!'
orig_question: Who is the publisher of Sauron?  the publisher refers to publisher_name; Sauron refers to superhero_name = 'Sauron'
orig_masked: Who is the [T7] of [V1]?  the [T7] refers to [C15]; [V1] refers to [C28] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
evidence: the author refers to au_lname; You Can Combat Computer Stress! refers to title = 'You Can Combat Computer Stress!'
orig_evidence: the publisher refers to publisher_name; Sauron refers to superhero_name = 'Sauron'
gold_sql: SELECT T1.au_lname FROM authors AS T1 INNER JOIN titleauthor as T2 ON T2.au_id = T1.au_id INNER JOIN titles AS T3 ON T2.title_id = T3.title_id WHERE T3.title = 'You Can Combat Computer Stress!'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.publisher_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Sauron'

question: State different movies who have movie release date before 1997 and received an amount of ratings greater than 100000
orig_question: State different accounts who have account opening date before 1997 and own an amount of money greater than 3000USD
orig_masked: State different [T1] who have [T1] [C2] [C2] [V2] and [C48] an [C48] of [C48] greater than [V1]  ; [V1] is a value of the column [C48]; [V2] is a value of the column [C2]
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT title FROM movie WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', "Release Date") < '1997' AND "Rating Count" > 100000
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.account_id FROM trans AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date) < '1997' AND T1.amount > 3000

question: What is the average home goals scored of the matches of Club Brugge? average home goals scored = AVG(FTHG);
orig_question: What is the average overall rating of the football player Aaron Doran? average overall rating = AVG(overall_rating);
orig_masked: What is the average [C153] of the [T4] [V1]? average [C153] = AVG([C153]);; [V1] is a value of the column [C126]
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(FTHG) FROM matchs WHERE HomeTeam = 'Club Brugge'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(t2.overall_rating) AS REAL) / COUNT(t2.id) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t1.player_name = 'Aaron Doran'

question: For the driver who shipped the biggest single weight, what was his/her first weight of shipment after starting the job?
orig_question: For the client who applied the biggest loan, what was his/her first amount of transaction after opened the account?
orig_masked: For the [T1] who [T6] the biggest [C34], what was his/her first [C48] of [T8] after [T1] the [T1]?
gold_sql: SELECT weight FROM shipment WHERE driver_id in (SELECT driver_id FROM shipment ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 1) ORDER BY ship_date ASC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.amount FROM loan AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN trans AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id ORDER BY T1.amount DESC, T3.date ASC LIMIT 1
note: I found this one hard to fit, this is my best effort but if you can think of something better I'm open to it.

question: What is the total number of actors without full name?  actors without full name refers to Name IS NULL
orig_question: What is the total number of superheroes without full name?  superheroes without full name refers to full_name IS NULL
orig_masked: What is the total number of [T9] without [C20]?  [T9] without [C20] refers to [C20] IS NULL
evidence: actors without full name refers to Name IS NULL
orig_evidence: superheroes without full name refers to full_name IS NULL
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(ActorID) FROM actor WHERE Name IS NULL
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(id) FROM superhero WHERE full_name IS NULL

question: How many movies were released in Action in 1996? Genre refers to movie genre; Action is one of movie genres.
orig_question: How many accounts were opened in Litomerice in 1996? A2 refers to district name; Litomerice is one of district names.
orig_masked: How many [T1] were [T5] in [V1] in [V2]? [C24] refers to [T5] [C24]; [V1] is one of [T5] [C24].; [V1] is a value of the column [C24]; [V2] is a value of the column [C2]
evidence: Genre refers to movie genre; Action is one of movie genres.
orig_evidence: A2 refers to district name; Litomerice is one of district names.
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT MovieID) FROM movie WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', "Release Date") = '1996' AND Genre = 'Action'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T2.account_id) FROM district AS T1 INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T2.date) = '1996' AND T1.A2 = 'Litomerice'

question: Give the car name of car with the lowest cylinders.  lowest cylinders refers to MIN(cylinders);
orig_question: Give the hero ID of superhero with the lowest attribute value.  lowest attribute value refers to MIN(attribute_value);
orig_masked: Give the [C11] of [T9] with the lowest [C10].  lowest [C10] refers to MIN([C10]);
orig_evidence: lowest attribute value refers to MIN(attribute_value);
gold_sql: SELECT car_name FROM data WHERE cylinders = ( SELECT MIN(cylinders) FROM data )
orig_gold_sql: SELECT hero_id FROM hero_attribute WHERE attribute_value = ( SELECT MIN(attribute_value) FROM hero_attribute )

question: What is the genre of film with film id 18?  genre refers to category.name where film_category.category_id = category.category_id;
orig_question: What is the eye colour of superhero with superhero ID 75?  eye colour refers to colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id;
orig_masked: What is the [C19] of [T9] with [C24] [V1]?  [C19] refers to [C5] where [C19] = [C6];; [V1] is a value of the column [C24]
evidence: genre refers to category.name where film_category.category_id = category.category_id;
orig_evidence: eye colour refers to colour where eye_colour_id = colour.id;
gold_sql: SELECT T3.name FROM film as T1 INNER JOIN film_category as T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id INNER JOIN category as T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id WHERE T1.film_id = 18
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T2.colour FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T1.id = 75

question: What is the ethnicity of the patient with maximum value? maximum value refers to MAX(observation.value);
orig_question: What is the race of the superhero with maximum attribute value? maximum attribute value refers to MAX(attribute_value);
orig_masked: What is the [C17] of the [T9] with maximum [C10]? maximum [C10] refers to MAX([C10]);
orig_evidence: maximum attribute value refers to MAX(attribute_value);
gold_sql: SELECT T1.ethnicity FROM patients AS T1 INNER JOIN observations AS T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient WHERE T2.value = (SELECT value FROM observations ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 1)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.race FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_attribute AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN race AS T3 ON T1.race_id = T3.id ORDER BY T2.attribute_value DESC LIMIT 1

question: List the titles of Abraham Lincoln.  Abraham Lincoln is the name of author; list the titles refers to book.title;
orig_question: List the powers of Hunter Zolomon.  Hunter Zolomon is the full name of superhero; list the powers refers to power_name;
orig_masked: List the [C31] of [V1].  [V1] is the [C20] of [T9]; list the [C31] refers to [C31];; [V1] is a value of the column [C20]
evidence: Abraham Lincoln is the name of author; list the titles refers to book.title;
orig_evidence: Hunter Zolomon is the full name of superhero; list the powers refers to power_name;
gold_sql: SELECT T3.title FROM author as T1 INNER JOIN book_author as T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id INNER JOIN book AS T3 ON T2.book_id = T3.book_id WHERE T1.author_name = 'Abraham Lincoln'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.power_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T2.power_id = T3.id WHERE T1.full_name = 'Hunter Zolomon'
